metadata.title: Unified Controls Framework (UCF)
metadata.description: A superset control taxonomy that maps controls across NIST 800-53, SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, CMMC L2, FedRAMP, GDPR, and ISO 42001. Each UCF domain represents a security/compliance concept with mappings to equivalent controls in each framework.

domains.title: Access Control
domains.description: Manage account creation, modification, disabling, and removal. Enforce least-privilege and separation of duties.

domains.title: Audit & Accountability
domains.description: Generate, review, and protect audit records to enable monitoring, analysis, and reporting of unlawful or unauthorized activity.

domains.title: Configuration Management
domains.description: Establish and maintain baseline configurations and inventories of organizational systems throughout their life cycles.

domains.title: Identification & Authentication
domains.description: Identify and authenticate users, processes, and devices before granting access to organizational systems.

domains.title: Incident Response
domains.description: Establish incident handling capabilities including preparation, detection, analysis, containment, recovery, and user response.

domains.title: System & Communications Protection
domains.description: Monitor, control, and protect communications at external and key internal boundaries. Implement subnetwork isolation.

domains.title: System & Information Integrity
domains.description: Identify, report, and correct system flaws in a timely manner. Provide protection from malicious code and monitor system security alerts.

domains.title: Risk Assessment
domains.description: Assess risk to organizational operations, assets, and individuals resulting from the operation of information systems.

domains.title: Personnel Security
domains.description: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access. Ensure personnel actions and transfers protect organizational information.

domains.title: Physical Protection
domains.description: Limit physical access to systems, equipment, and operating environments. Protect and monitor the physical facility.

domains.title: Awareness & Training
domains.description: Ensure personnel are trained and aware of security risks associated with their activities and applicable policies.

domains.title: Contingency Planning
domains.description: Establish, maintain, and implement plans for emergency response, backup operations, and post-disaster recovery.

domains.title: Cryptography & Key Management
domains.description: Establish cryptographic standards, manage keys across their lifecycle, and ensure data is encrypted in transit and at rest.

domains.title: Data Protection & Privacy
domains.description: Classify, label, and safeguard sensitive data. Implement data-loss prevention and privacy controls for PII/PHI.

domains.title: Vulnerability Management
domains.description: Continuously scan for vulnerabilities, prioritize by risk, and remediate within defined SLAs.

domains.title: Change Management
domains.description: Control changes to systems and software through formal approval, testing, and rollback procedures.

domains.title: Logging & Monitoring
domains.description: Centralize log collection, establish alerting thresholds, and ensure real-time monitoring of security events.

domains.title: Supply Chain Risk Management
domains.description: Assess third-party risk, enforce contractual security requirements, and monitor supplier compliance continuously.

domains.title: Media Protection
domains.description: Control physical and digital media access, storage, transport, sanitization, and disposal.

domains.title: Maintenance
domains.description: Perform controlled maintenance on systems, require authorization for non-local maintenance, and supervise maintenance personnel.

domains.title: Network Access Control
domains.description: Segment networks, enforce firewall rules, and restrict network access to authorized traffic flows.

domains.title: Secure Development
domains.description: Embed security into the SDLC with secure coding standards, code review, SAST/DAST, and separation of environments.
